articles.title: 8 students hospitalized in pepper spraying at South Shore high school
articles.description:
Eights students were hospitalized Tuesday after a student used pepper spray during a fight at South Shore International College Prep High School, according to Chicago police.

Around noon, an unknown number of students were in a fight when one used pepper spray, which affected 10 students, police said. Eight of them were taken to University of Chicago Medical Center in good condition, police said.

The school didn't immediately respond to a request for comment. Police were investigating the incident.

articles.title: Hot-air balloon with 13 people lands in California back yard - video

Resident Hunter Perrin said he was 'very confused' when a neighbour alerted him and his wife that they had visitors. Passengers on the balloon said the pilot informed them he needed to make an emergency landing due to low fuel and a shift in winds. After passengers disembarked, the pilot landed the balloon nearby in the street, where it was dismantled Continue reading...

articles.title: Will Miami Beach limit towing of residents’ cars? City commissioner has a plan
articles.description: A wrecker tows into Tremont Towing company 1916 Bay Road, Miami Beach, Wednesday, May 4, 2011. In a city where scarce parking is a perennial issue, Miami Beach's towing companies are locked in a battle for control of the lucrative world of public tows - and the city is stuck in the middle. The photographer watched this yellow wrecker and it took 13-14 minutes for it to return twice with two more cars.

articles.title: MCPO nets most wanted suspect, seizes P200K worth of shabu in weekly operations
articles.description: MANDAUE CITY, Cebu — The Mandaue City Police Office (MCPO) recorded the arrest of one of its most wanted persons and the seizure of over P200,000 worth of suspected shabu as part of its weekly accomplishments from April 13 to 19, 2026. MCPO Director, Police Colonel Cirilo Acosta Jr., said a buy-bust operation on April 14 in Sitio Dunggoan, Barangay Basak, led to the arrest of a suspect identified as Anthony. The suspect, a habal-habal driver residing in Barangay Basak, was caught with around 25 grams of shabu with an estimated value of P170,000. Police also recovered a firearm. READ: [...]... articles.title: Attacks on Health Care in Sudan, 01-14 April 2026
articles.description: Country: Sudan Source: Insecurity Insight Please refer to the attached file. Insecurity Insight identified 735 incidents of violence against or obstruction of access to health care in Sudan since fighting started between the Sudan Armed Forces (SAF) and Rapid Support Forces (RSF) in April 2023 and 31 March 2026. At least 195 health workers were killed and 126 arrested, while health facilities were reportedly damaged on 205 occasions. Most of these 735 incidents were attributed to the RSF. View the incidents on our interactive map . Download the data . Updated every Monday and includes information on weapons use, perpetrators and effects. For events descriptions and data enquiries, please get in touch . Numbers may change if or when further information is made available. See here for methodology. Food security is a major concern in Sudan. Between February and May 2026, 19.1 million people are projected to experience high levels of acute food insecurity (IPC Phase 3 or above). Insecurity Insight’s monitoring of violent events showed an alarmingly high rate of conflict incidents affecting the food system, with violence at markets as well as incidents of cattle looting and killing and attacks on civilians growing food, selling or buying food reported in high numbers. Health needs are also high throughout the country. As of March 2026, two out of three people in Sudan no longer have access to essential health services and 80% of hospitals are out of service in affected areas. The lack of protection of vital civilian infrastructure is a driver of escalating humanitarian needs across Sudan. This brief identifies trends and recurring patterns, highlights how the conduct of hostilities contributes to creating humanitarian needs, and provides insights into anticipating them. It also provides a list of actionable and practical recommendations in service of sustaining international action to mitigate harm. Documented incidents 01-14 April 2026 The publicly reported incidents below are not a complete nor a representative list of all incidents that affected the provision of health care between 01-14 April 2026. The incidents below have not been verified through ground investigations. There is a delay in reporting incidents due to our open source verification protocol. 01 April 2026: In Kosti/Rabak town, Al Jabalian district, White Nile state, the Central Medicine Warehouse was hit and destroyed by a drone, injuring a pharmacist and two warehouse workers. Sources: Sudan Tribune , UN News and WHO 01 April 2026: In Khor Ramla area, Gharb Jabal Marrah locality, Central Darfur state, the tyres of an International Medical Corps vehicle were reportedly shot at by an unidentified armed group, and the crew’s belongings looted. Source: ACLED 1 02 April 2026: In Al Nadhir neighbourhood, Al-Daein city, East Darfur state, the Family Hospital was raided and looted by a force affiliated with the RSF members, who assaulted and expelled health workers and patients. The facility and medical equipment were also damaged. Sources: Sudan Doctors Network , Sudan Tribune and WHO 02 April 2026: In Al Jabalain city, White Nile state, Al-Jabalain Hospital was hit by two RSF drone strikes, damaging the operating theatre and the maternity ward, killing at least 12 people, including ten health workers, two of whom were doctors. The attack also injured 22 others. Sources: MSF , MSF Sudan , Sudan Tribune Sudan War Updates , Info Bae , Info Bae I , New Age , and WHO 09 April 2026: In An Nuhud town and district, West Kordofan state, a hospital was reportedly hit by an SAF drone strike, killing and injuring an unspecified number of people. Source: ACLED 1
